text: Increased plasma estradiol and resulting precocious puberty have been studied in laboratory mammals by addition of hormones (Piekarski et al. 2017), toxicants with endocrine disrupting properties (Li et al. 2018; Gan et al. 2024), and modifying diet (Bo et al. 2022). Studies involving doses of laboratory mammals with various forms of estradiol (e.g. 17beta-estradiol) are supportive of the mechanism of increases in exposure to estradiol compounds causing precocious puberty (Piekarski et al. 2017).  Estradiol triggers development and maturation of reproductive organs during puberty, causing growth in the vagina and uterus from increasing proliferation and differentiation of cells.
